# VAE


<!-- WARNING: THIS FILE WAS AUTOGENERATED! DO NOT EDIT! -->

## Overview

This page documents the core VAE utilities used by `qdisc.vae.core`.

It presents the main tools behind the module:

- [`VAEmodel`](https://qic-ibk.github.io/qdisc/lib_nbs/vae/core.html#vaemodel):
  the encoder-decoder VAE wrapper that produces latent variables and
  decoder outputs.
- reconstruction losses for discrete, continuous and hybrid quantum
  data.
- [`VAETrainer`](https://qic-ibk.github.io/qdisc/lib_nbs/vae/core.html#vaetrainer):
  a training helper that applies the VAE objective, tracks training
  history, and visualizes latent structure.

### VAE model wrapper

[`VAEmodel`](https://qic-ibk.github.io/qdisc/lib_nbs/vae/core.html#vaemodel)
composes an encoder and decoder module. The encoder maps inputs to a
Gaussian latent distribution `(mean, logvar)`, the model samples latent
variables using the reparameterization trick, and the decoder converts
these latent variables back to output conditional probabilities.

This wrapper makes it easy to use different encoder/decoder
architectures while keeping a standard VAE interface.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/qic-ibk/qdisc/blob/main/qdisc/vae/core.py#L126"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### VAEmodel

``` python

def VAEmodel(
    encoder:Module, decoder:Module, parent:Union=<flax.linen.module._Sentinel object at 0x7f35dc502060>,
    name:Optional=None
)->None:

```

*VAE model, wrapper calling the encoder -\> reparam -\> decoder*

Args:

    encoder: nn.Module
    decoder: nn.Module

Returns:

    mean: (batch, latent_dim)
    logvar: (batch, latent_dim)
    z: (batch, latent_dim)
    cp: (batch, seq_len, local_dimension)

## Trainer

[`VAETrainer`](https://qic-ibk.github.io/qdisc/lib_nbs/vae/core.html#vaetrainer)
manages optimization, batching, and training history for the VAE.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/qic-ibk/qdisc/blob/main/qdisc/vae/core.py#L178"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### VAETrainer

``` python

def VAETrainer(
    model:Module, dataset:Dataset, optimizer:GradientTransformation=adabelief
):

```

*Training wrapper for a the cpVAE for quantum*

Args:

    model: nn.Module (VAEmodel)

    dataset: Dataset

    optimizer: optax.GradientTransformation

VAE:

### VAETrainer.train

``` python

def train(
    num_epochs:int, batch_size:int, key:PRNGKey, learning_rate:float=0.001, beta:float=1.0, gamma:float=0,
    alpha:float=0.0, printing_rate:int=1, re_shuffle:bool=True
):

```

*High-level training looop.*

Args:

    num_epochs: int
    batch_size: int
    key: jax.random.PRNGKey
    learning_rate: float
    beta: float KL weight
    gamma: float TC weight
    alpha: float weight for the two part of the hybrid loss
    printing_rate: int how often to print info during training
    re_shuffle: bool if we reshuffle the dataset to create batches at each epoch

Returns:

    None
